name: Spicy Party Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 4 Anaheim chili peppers, roasted, seeded, and peeled
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the softened cream cheese and shredded Monterey Jack. Beat with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
2. Add the first 8 ounces of sour cream to the mixture. Beat until well combined.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating after each addition until just incorporated.
4. Stir in the salsa and chili peppers (or canned green chile). Mix until thoroughly combined.
5. Pour the batter into a prepared 9-inch springform pan. Place the pan on a baking sheet for easy removal.
6. Bake at 350°F for 40-45 minutes, or until the center is nearly set.
7. Immediately spread any remaining sour cream over the top of the cheesecake.
8. Remove from the oven and let cool completely on a rack.
9. Chill in the refrigerator for 3 to 4 hours before serving.
10. To serve, carefully remove the cheesecake from the pan. Garnish with chopped tomato, tortilla chips, and fresh herbs like chives, cilantro, and parsley.
